title: Porcelia parviflora
intro: Porcelia parviflora, also known as the small-flowered porcelia, is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Porcelia parviflora is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies like syrups and cough medicines.
taxonomy: Porcelia parviflora is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Porcelia, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The classification of Porcelia parviflora places it within the order Magnoliales. Further classification groups it into the class Magnoliopsida. Within the kingdom Plantae, Porcelia parviflora is a member of the phylum Magnoliophyta.
